title: quercus pisiformis
intro: Quercus pisiformis, also known as the Mexican Buckeye, is a small tree or shrub native to Mexico. It is a member of the beech family and is valued for its medicinal properties. The bark and leaves of the Mexican Buckeye contain tannins, which have been traditionally used to treat fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Quercus pisiformis have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems and inflammation, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
